title: The 45-Minute Weekly Reset for Solo Operators
excerpt: A practical cadence to clear bottlenecks and plan your highest-leverage work.
body: Start with a hard stop on open loops: unanswered messages, unassigned tasks, and draft commitments.
Then pick your top three outcomes for the next week and map them to specific execution blocks.
End by identifying one workflow bottleneck and one process improvement for next week.

title: How to Build SOPs You’ll Actually Reuse
excerpt: A straightforward method for writing SOPs that are clear, short, and usable.
body: SOPs fail when they are too long, too abstract, or detached from real tools.
Write steps at decision points, not every click. Include sample outputs and edge cases.
Review SOPs after 2-3 executions and trim anything no one actually uses.

title: From Tool Overload to One Operating Stack
excerpt: Use this decision framework to cut redundant apps and simplify your workflow.
body: List all current tools by workflow stage: capture, plan, execute, review.
For each stage, keep one primary source of truth and one optional specialist tool.
Migrate active projects first and archive legacy systems with a clear freeze date.

primaryCta.label: Use the Tool Stack Audit Checklist

title: AI Prompts That Improve Execution, Not Just Ideas
excerpt: Prompt patterns designed for planning, handoffs, and follow-through.
body: Use prompts to reduce ambiguity in weekly plans, handoffs, and client updates.
Design prompts around deliverables and deadlines rather than brainstorming alone.
Track which prompts improve cycle time and standardize the winners into SOPs.

title: The Solo Operator Planning System (Capture → Execute)
excerpt: A complete operating loop for running projects without daily chaos.
body: Capture all incoming requests into one intake layer, not scattered inboxes.
Convert requests into a weekly plan with constraints and realistic throughput.
Review outcomes every week and evolve one small part of the system at a time.

title: Client Delivery Playbook: Keep Work Moving Every Week
excerpt: A repeatable playbook for managing scope, updates, and delivery rhythm.
body: Define weekly client update windows and reuse one update template every cycle.
Pair each deliverable with owner, due date, and next verification checkpoint.
Escalate blockers in 24 hours and document decisions directly in project context.
